title: 控方证人
summary:
Dr. Ross and Mr. Anstruther visit Mary-Annichelle, a woman referred to as "the Auntie," who lives in a state of confusion and believes her dreams are more real than life. During the visit, Anstruther mentions a destroyed monastery and a wall marked with a "Death Hound" shape that terrifies local farmers. Upon hearing this, Mary-Annichelle becomes hysterical, claiming her memories are true. She recounts using a specific power to unleash the "death hound" on people who ignored her warnings at an altar. 

After Dr. Ross administers a liquid to calm her, Mary-Annichelle describes a circular city consisting of three rings for gods, priestesses, and priests, with a "house of crystal" at its center. As she mentions the crystal house, she traces circles on her forehead before suddenly regaining her composure and forgetting her words.

Outside, Anstruther suggests Mary-Annichelle is deranged, but Ross posits that what appears supernatural might actually be natural laws not yet understood, such as the ability to exert destructive force through willpower. Ross reveals he has been conducting experiments with crystals, noting that a previous patient reacted to a crystal by kneeling and claiming that "faith still exists" before passing out. Ross intends to conduct a further experiment the following day by having a patient gaze at a crystal, a topic that causes him to become uncharacteristically stiff and formal when questioned by Anstruther.
